---
title: "Soaked and Cooked Chickpeas (for hummus)"
description: "How to soak and cook dried chickpeas for hummus. Yields about 1 kg cooked chickpeas; uses bicarbonate of soda to speed cooking and ensure an even, tender texture."
category: "Recipe"
tags:
  - "chickpeas"
searchTags:
  - "Recipe"
  - "Chickpeas"
baseYield: 1
yieldLabel: "Makes approx. 1 kg cooked chickpeas"
heroTitle: "Preparing the chickpeas"
heroCopy: "Soak dried chickpeas overnight, then simmer with a little bicarbonate of soda until very soft — perfect for hummus."
ingredientsHeading: "Ingredients"
methodHeading: "Method"
notesHeading: "Notes"
photoAlt: "Cooked chickpeas in a bowl"
primaryPhoto: ""
thumbnailPhoto: "data:image/jpeg;base64,"
fallbackPhoto: "data:image/jpeg;base64,"
photoCreditText: ""
photoCreditUrl: ""
ingredients:
  - 
    id: "dried-chickpeas"
    name: "dried chickpeas"
    quantity: 500
    unit: "g"
    format:
      decimals: 0
      fractions: true
  - 
    id: "bicarbonate-of-soda"
    name: "bicarbonate of soda"
    quantity: 0.5
    unit: "tsp"
    format:
      decimals: 2
      fractions: true
steps:
  - "Put [[dried-chickpeas|quantity=500]] in a large bowl and cover with cold water to twice their depth, leaving room above the water for the [[dried-chickpeas]] to expand. Leave to soak overnight for at least 14 hours but no more than 24 hours, changing the water halfway through soaking."
  - "Drain and rinse the soaked [[dried-chickpeas]] well, then transfer them to a large saucepan. Cover with fresh cold water and bring to the boil."
  - "Boil the [[dried-chickpeas]] for 10 minutes, then reduce the heat to a steady simmer."
  - "Carefully add [[bicarbonate-of-soda|quantity=0.5]] to the simmering [[dried-chickpeas]] (it will froth). Continue to simmer the [[dried-chickpeas]] until they are really soft when pressed or smashed between your fingers. Start checking from about 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes; depending on the [[dried-chickpeas]] this can take up to 2 hours."
  - "When the [[dried-chickpeas]] are very soft, drain them to yield about 1 kg cooked, drained [[dried-chickpeas]]. Cool before using in hummus or store in the fridge."
notes:
  - "Bicarbonate of soda helps the chickpeas become evenly tender and speeds up cooking, but add it carefully because the water will froth."
  - "Test readiness by taking a chickpea and smashing it between your fingers — it should be really, really soft."
  - "Soaked then cooked chickpeas are ideal for hummus; chilling the cooked chickpeas before blending gives a firmer texture if required."
---
